rationale: The citing paper explicitly invokes Homburg et al. (2020) when introducing a multiagency perspective to justify that suppliers are governed simultaneously by two actors (the buying group and the buyer). This idea is then used in the paper's conceptual setup and hypothesis logic about cross-level governance interactions, not just in a background citation list. Although the cited paper's methods are not reused, its theoretical lens materially informs how the citing paper frames its core mechanism.
relation_description: The citing study applies the cited paper's multiagency perspective to a different B2B setting, using it to theorize parallel governance by buying groups and buyers over suppliers. It builds new interaction hypotheses in triadic buyer-group-supplier relationships based on that transferred logic.
